meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client builds. Learn how to implement scalable SaaS pricing strategies and ship faster using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is often feature creep stemming from vague pricing models. By implementing value-based SaaS pricing, you force scope discipline. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing infrastructure and Claude Code to automate the integration, ensuring you ship a profitable, production-ready MVP in record time.
content.intro:
You’re staring at the cursor again. The client's project is a sprawling, half-finished mess of 'nice-to-haves' because you never defined the boundaries of the build. In the freelancer world, scope creep isn't just an annoyance; it’s the silent killer of your profitability and your sanity. You’re trading hours for anxiety, and the finish line keeps moving further away.

But what if your pricing model was the tool that actually finished the project for you? By architecting your SaaS pricing as a strict technical constraint, you turn your billable hours into a roadmap for delivery. It’s time to stop coding into the void and start building with the precision of a lead engineer. Let’s ship.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Stop charging by the hour. Define three distinct tiers that map directly to specific technical modules. This forces the client to choose scope, effectively limiting your development surface area.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a 3-tier SaaS pricing model including features for 'Starter', 'Pro', and 'Enterprise'. Map these to a modular architecture where each tier unlocks specific API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate pricing-schema.json based on the defined 3-tier structure'

steps.title: Automate Billing Logic Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate for Stripe integration. By automating the boilerplate, you avoid the 'blank page' syndrome that stalls most developers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure Express.js controller for Stripe Checkout sessions, including logic for subscription webhooks and user-tier verification middleware.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install stripe && claude add-feature billing-controller'

steps.title: Integrate Feature-Gating Middleware
steps.description: Implement code-level enforcement of your pricing tiers. If a feature isn't in the user's tier, the code shouldn't even execute. This ensures your project stays focused.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript middleware function 'requireTier(tierLevel)' that checks the user's subscription status from the database and returns a 403 if the tier is insufficient.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'implement requireTier middleware across all protected routes'

steps.title: Rapid UI Component Mapping
steps.description: Use your pricing model to toggle UI components. This prevents over-building UI that the user isn't paying for, saving you hours of front-end development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React HOC (Higher Order Component) 'TierGuard' that conditionally renders components based on the user's current pricing tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'wrap premium components with TierGuard in Dashboard.tsx'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Finalize the production environment. Use Claude Code to run sanity checks on your billing flow before shipping to the client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite for the billing workflow, simulating a user upgrading from Starter to Pro and verifying the feature access updates instantly.

pro_tips: Always include a 'Custom/Enterprise' tier that acts as a catch-all for scope creep; charge a premium for the flexibility.
Use database-level constraints for tier access so that even if the UI is bypassed, the API remains secure.
Ship the billing logic first. If the client can't pay, you shouldn't be building.
Automate your invoice generation using the same data structures that feed your pricing page to ensure 100% accuracy.
Keep a 'Features Graveyard' document; if a client asks for a feature not in their tier, put it there to satisfy them without building it.
content.next_steps: Once the billing infrastructure is live, conduct a 'Scope Review' with your client to prune any features that don't fit the defined tiers, then proceed to iterative deployment.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting projects drift into the abyss of unfinished code. By aligning your technical architecture with your pricing model, you regain control over your time and your deliverables. The AI Builder community is here to ensure you never miss a deadline again. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
